title: JSON Schema Draft-04 to TypeBox Converter Rules

decisionIntegration.B2: additionalProperties NOT set to false by default; unknown fields flow through
decisionIntegration.B3: Generated code lives in src/lib/generated; public surface via src/lib/schemas.ts compat re-exports
decisionIntegration.B4: Output uses sorted keys and deterministic property ordering for byte-stable diffs
decisionIntegration.B5: External refs (geojson.org) kept as Type.Any() with explicit inline comment; allowlist maintained in manifest

idStrategy.namingConvention: camelCase definition key → PascalCase export name (numberValue → NumberValue)

typeMapping.notes: Passthrough constraints: format, pattern, minLength, maxLength
Passthrough constraints: minimum, maximum, exclusiveMinimum, exclusiveMaximum, multipleOf

typeMapping.typeboxOutput: Type.Object({ ... }) with required keys as plain, optional as Type.Optional()
typeMapping.notes: additionalProperties NOT set false by default (decision B2)
Gate A decision: nullable semantics via union
Foo must be declared with $id before use; same-file definition

typeMapping.typeboxOutput: Type.Ref(Foo) with import from generated foundation module
typeMapping.notes: Resolver maps path to module; $id on target must be stable
Semantic difference from oneOf is ignored; validation passes on any match

typeMapping.typeboxOutput: Type.Union([S1, S2, ...]) with additionalProperties:false on dominated branches (preferred) OR plain Type.Union + EXCEPTION if pattern not applicable
typeMapping.notes: TypeBox has no strict oneOf. When all branches are plain objects (no $ref/combiners), exclusivity is restored by closing 'dominated' branches (those whose property key set is a strict subset of another branch's key set) with additionalProperties:false. This is the preferred path: no semantic-loss exception, no custom validator. Falls back to plain Type.Union + semantic-loss exception when branches include $ref, anyOf, allOf, enum, or other non-object structures. Validated against Signal K meta.displayScale (8/8 exclusivity cases pass).
For mixed-type enums (string + number), each literal preserves its inferred type
For specific regex patterns (not .*), use Type.Record(Type.RegExp(pattern), S)
Decision B5: external refs become Type.Any() with inline comment; URL added to external-ref-allowlist in manifest

droppedKeywords.reason: Draft-04 local id; replaced by stable $id formula
Not supported in TypeBox Array; ignored unless false
TypeBox emits its own schema declaration
Upstream editorial comment; stripped and logged as warning

outputConventions.fileNaming: {batch-folder}/{definitionName}.ts for discrete definitions; index.ts barrel per folder
outputConventions.propertyOrdering: alphabetical within Type.Object to ensure byte-stable output (decision B4)
outputConventions.headerComment: // Generated from SignalK specification {upstreamRef} — do not edit manually

exceptionPolicy.semanticLoss: Log to manifest.exceptions[]; continue generation; add inline comment in output
exceptionPolicy.unresolvedRef: Emit Type.Unknown() with inline comment; fail parity check; block batch promotion
exceptionPolicy.missingDefinition: Emit Type.Unknown() with inline comment; fail parity check; block batch promotion

checks: Type.Static output matches expected shape for 3 sample paths
